top_weaknesses.issue: Reads as polished analyst/VC memo rather than first-person operator observation; missing one concrete enterprise buying story or named procurement decision
top_weaknesses.fix: Insert one specific anecdote (named buyer, named category, real before/after) early in the piece

top_weaknesses.issue: Heavy load of consensus phrasing ('innovation theater', 'serious deployments', 'fewer vendors, larger contracts, tighter permissions') reads as AI/strategy boilerplate
top_weaknesses.fix: Cut three-part-symmetry summary lines; replace with one sharp, non-obvious claim (e.g. which agent categories consolidate first)

top_weaknesses.issue: First 155 chars open with context ('first enterprise reaction was experimentation') instead of the consolidation thesis
top_weaknesses.fix: Move the 'how many they can remove' beat into the lede so the snippet carries the argument

top_weaknesses.issue: Underuses search-intent phrases like 'AI agent vendor consolidation' / 'agent sprawl'
top_weaknesses.fix: Surface 'agent sprawl' explicitly in body and once in an H2 lead

consensus_summary: The H2 spine narrates a coherent argument and the structure is mobile-safe; all four personas can follow the thesis. It reads, however, like a competent VC memo without a surprising mechanism, named example, or first-person edge — strong enough to ship but unlikely to be shared by the HN/Stratechery crowd.
